skill.name: qwen-image-2-pro
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI, with strong support for text-heavy visual designs.
skill.summary: Generate text-rich images and edits with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, network, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found that the weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from words in documentation, while the external command and network findings are legitimate but expected for an inference.sh image generation skill. No prompt injection attempt, credential theft, obfuscated payload, or unauthorized data exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: External Inference CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run infsh commands. This is expected for the skill, but it executes an external CLI that can submit prompts, options, and image references to a hosted inference service. The allowed-tools declaration narrows intended shell use to infsh, and no arbitrary shell construction or hidden script was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically central to the skill. Confidence is high that external CLI execution occurs, but no command injection or malicious command behavior was identified.

medium_findings.title: Network Submission to Hosted Image Service
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on inference.sh and documented app endpoints. Prompts and reference image URLs may be sent to external services, which creates privacy and compliance considerations for sensitive images or confidential text.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly references inference.sh and reference image URLs. This is legitimate functionality, but the data-sharing risk is clear from the documented workflow.

low_findings.title: Installer Command Mentioned in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill mentions npx skills add commands for installing related skills. These are user-facing examples, not hidden execution, but users should verify package sources before running installer commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The installer commands are visible documentation examples. They could execute package tooling if copied or invoked, but no concealed invocation path was found in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words such as description or design-related text, not cryptographic APIs. No hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm use was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text and parameter tables at the cited lines, not cryptographic code. The evidence strongly supports false positive classification.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: The static system reconnaissance alert does not correspond to host inspection or environment discovery. The cited line documents a negative_prompt field for image generation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a parameter description and contains no system command or reconnaissance behavior. This makes the static alert very likely to be a false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Permission for infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: The frontmatter grants Bash access for infsh commands. This permission is necessary for the skill, but it means marketplace users should understand that generation requests leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools entry is direct evidence of command permission. The risk is limited to infsh rather than arbitrary shell commands in the declared permission.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reference Image URL Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The image editing example accepts external image URLs. This can expose private image locations or cause the service to fetch user-provided URLs, so sensitive assets should be avoided.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example explicitly documents reference_images with URI fields. The security concern is contextual and privacy-focused, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Creating posters, banners, and edited images with readable text can require careful model settings. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro using inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro generation through the inference.sh CLI.
Creates text-to-image prompts for posters, banners, logos, and product scenes.
Supports text-heavy prompts with width, height, negative prompt, and prompt extension settings.
Documents multi-image generation with a num_images option.
Shows reference image editing workflows with one to three input image URLs.
Includes Python SDK examples for batch runs and streamed progress updates.
limitations: Requires an installed and authenticated inference.sh CLI or compatible SDK setup.
Sends prompts and reference image URLs to an external hosted service.
Output quality depends on model behavior and prompt clarity.
The skill is documentation only and does not include local validation scripts.

use_cases.title: Design Text-Heavy Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Generate posters or sale banners with exact headline text, dimensions, and negative prompts for clearer typography.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Create several image concepts for product launches, mood boards, or stakeholder reviews before commissioning final artwork.

use_cases.title: Automate Image Generation Runs
use_cases.description: Use CLI or Python SDK examples to run repeatable image jobs with seeds, image counts, and streamed progress.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2-pro to generate one 1024 by 1024 image of a clean product photo for a ceramic mug on a white desk.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Poster with Exact Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a vertical poster with the exact title "SPRING WORKSHOP" in large green serif text. Add the subtitle "April 12" below it. Disable prompt extension.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Campaign Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create four 1920 by 1080 banner variations for a summer sale. Use clean product photography style and avoid blurry or distorted text.

prompt_templates.title: Edit with Reference Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Use two reference image URLs to place the outfit from Image 2 on the person from Image 1. Return two realistic edited results.

output_examples.input: A request for a square product image of a watch with a clean studio background.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an inference.sh run for Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro with a concise product photography prompt and default image size.

output_examples.input: A request for a vertical event poster with exact headline and date text.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses a 1024 by 1536 layout, quotes the required text, disables prompt extension, and adds a negative prompt for blurry text.

output_examples.input: A request to create several banner concepts for a promotion.
output_examples.output: The assistant sets a wide banner size, requests multiple images, and describes the expected visual style and text placement.

best_practices: Quote any exact words that must appear in the generated image.
Set dimensions and prompt extension behavior before running text-heavy jobs.
Avoid private or regulated content in prompts and reference image URLs.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential mockups, unreleased product images, or private portraits without approval.
Do not expect perfect text rendering without clear font, color, size, and position instructions.
Do not run installer or CLI commands from untrusted sources without reviewing them first.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates and edits images with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it best for text in images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation focuses on posters, banners, and other text-heavy designs.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses inference.sh and sends generation requests to an external service.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents reference image inputs for image editing and style transfer workflows.

faq.question: What should users avoid?
faq.answer: Users should avoid sending sensitive prompts, private image URLs, or confidential brand assets without approval.
